rulings.Case Title: NETLINE CORPORATION, VS. LOOMIS GROUP INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Judgment To Add Alter Ego Judgment Debtor
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, September 3, 2013, Line 8, PLAINTIFF NETLINE CORPORATION'S, Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Judgment To Add Alter Ego Judgment Debtor Plaintiff's motion to amend the judgment to add Jeffrey Loomis as an alter ego is denied. Plaintiff has not established that Jeffrey Loomis had control over the litigation of judgment debtor. (See NEC Electronics Inc. v. Hurt, 208 Cal. App. 3d 772, 778 (1989); Declaration of Jonich, 5-6; Declaration of Richardson in Opposition to Plaintiff's Motion to Amend Judgment to Add Alter Ego Judgment Debtor, 3-9.) Plaintiff has not produced sufficient evidence to meet the multi-factor alter ego test that there was a unity of interest between Jeffrey Loomis and judgment debtor, and that inequitable results will follow if the corporate separateness is respected. (See Zoran Corp. v. Chen, 185 Cal. App. 4th 799, 811 (2010).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE (A PSEUDONYM) VS. THE ARCHDIOCESE OF SAN FRANCISCO, AN ENTITY FORM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Compel Disco
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, September 3, 2013, Line 2, PLAINTIFF JANE DOE'S (A PSEUDONYM) MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Compel Discovery Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion denied; request for sanctions denied. Plaintiff has failed to file a supplemental separate statement pursuant to this Court's order of 8/16/13, and the Court presumes that the supplemental responses that were served by Defendant on 8/20/13 are sufficient. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: ILEANA A. TORUNO VS. BLUEPRINT STUDIOS TRENDS INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Compel Deft To Provide
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, September 3, 2013, Line 3, DEFENDANT BLUEPRINT STUDIOS TRENDS INC'S Motion To Compel Defendant To Provide Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The Court requests oral argument on what it believes to be the central issue of this motion, i.e. is the discovery of the names of other independent contractors retained by Defendant at times other than when Plaintiff was employed relevant to Plaintiff's claims in this litigation? Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: AMELIA VALDEZ VS. ERNESTO AGUIRRE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Special Interoggs
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, September 3, 2013, Line 4, DEFENDANT ERNESTO AGUIRRE's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interoggatories Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The Court requests oral argument on: (1) whether Plaintiff's profits from the business at issue are relevant as to Defendant's damages if the contract is rescinded, and (2) the scope of Plaintiff's right to privacy of financial matters. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing.

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT HALF INTERNATIONAL, INC. VS. ATEL FINANCIAL SERVICES, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, September 3, 2013, Line 9, DEFENDANT ATEL FINANCIAL SERVICES, LLC'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND The motion is granted. Even if the Court assumes that service was proper, Defendant has just cause for relief under CCP 473.5 because Mr. Morais declares at paragraph 9 of his declaration that defendant lacked actual knowledge of the suit prior to the entry of default. Defendant may file and serve an answer to the Complaint within 20 days after entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ERNICE FAGAN VS. KING AMERICAN AMBULANCE SERVICE et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, September 3, 2013, Line 10, DEFENDANT ALLIED MEDICAL SERVICES DBA DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Hearing required. The Court is inclined to sustain the demurrer without leave to amend under CCP 430.10(e). The complaint fails to state a cause of action. Plaintiff lacks standing to maintain this action under CCP 367 because she is not the real party in interest. Plaintiff complains about an injury to Mr. Baumel, but does not allege facts showing how she is his successor and can maintain this action. The alleged common law marriage does not create standing unless Plaintiff can allege that the common law marriage was validly contracted in another state. See Knight v. Superior Court (2005) 128 Cal. App. 4th 14, 19. The durable power of attorney does not create standing. The Court rejects defendant's statute of limitations argument. The complaint does not clearly disclose the date of injury. While defendant appeared at the premises on April 27, 2011, it is unclear when the injury (i.e., Mr. Baumel's death) occurred. =(302/MJM)
